On the first day on which both subsection 110(1) of the other Act and section 153 of this Act are in force, subsection 17(1) of the French version of the Hazardous Materials Information Review Act is replaced by the following:

S’il ne reçoit pas l’engagement signé ou n’est pas convaincu que le demandeur l’a respecté, l’agent de contrôle lui ordonne de se conformer aux dispositions de la Loi sur les produits dangereux, du Code canadien du travail ou de la loi de mise en oeuvre, selon le cas, sauf dans la mesure où elles obligeraient le demandeur à communiquer les renseignements visés par la demande, selon les modalités de forme et de temps précisées par l’ordre.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Division, other than sections 160 and 161, come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Paragraph 3(2)(h) of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of wine, beer or spirits from a province by an individual, if the individual brings the wine, beer or spirits or causes them to be brought into another province, in quantities and as permitted by the laws of the other province, for his or her personal consumption, and not for resale or other commercial use.

Section.Text: Paragraph 13(d) of the Judges Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the 144 puisne judges of the Superior Court, $288,100 each.

Section.Text: Paragraph 20(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the 57 other Justices of the Court of Queen’s Bench, $288,100 each.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Members of Parliament Retiring Allowances Act

Section.Text: The Members of Parliament Retiring Allowances Act is amended by adding the following after section 2.8:

If a member is suspended from the Senate or House of Commons, as the case may be, by a majority vote of that House, the member’s pensionable service is not to include the period that begins on the day on which the suspension starts and ends on the day fixed by a majority vote of that House for the reinstatement of the member’s entitlement to accrue pensionable service.
Despite any provision of Part I, Part II or Part V, no contribution shall be paid by a member under those Parts in respect of any period referred to in section 2.9.

Subsection.Text: No election shall be made under Part I or Part II by a member during any period that begins on the day on which the suspension referred to in section 2.9 starts and ends on the later of the day on which a majority vote is passed by the Senate or House of Commons, as the case may be, which reinstates the member’s entitlement to accrue pensionable service and the day fixed by that vote for that reinstatement.
The period referred to in subsection (1) is not to be included in the determination of the time limit for making an election under subsection 10(1) or 32(1).
No election shall be made under this Act in respect of any period referred to in section 2.9.

Section.Text: If, on the day on which this Division comes into force, a person has been suspended from the Senate or House of Commons, as the case may be, by a majority vote of that House and has not had their right to accrue pensionable service reinstated by such a vote, the periods referred to in section 2.9 and subsection 2.92(1) of the Members of Parliament Retiring Allowances Act, as enacted by section 166, begin on the day on which this Division comes into force.
Section 17 of the National Defence Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Canadian Forces shall consist of those of the following elements that are from time to time organized by or under the authority of the Minister:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: commands, including the Royal Canadian Navy, the Canadian Army and the Royal Canadian Air Force;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: formations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: units; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: other elements.

Subsection.Text: A unit or other element organized under subsection (1), other than a command or a formation, shall from time to time be embodied in a component of the Canadian Forces as directed by or under the authority of the Minister.

Section.MarginalNote: R.S., c. 31 (1st Supp.), s. 60 (Sch. I, s. 7)
Section.Text: Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Ranks of officers and non-commissioned members

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, the ranks of the officers and non-commissioned members of the Canadian Forces shall be as set out in the schedule.

A person holding a rank set out in the schedule shall use, or be referred to by, a designation of rank prescribed in regulations made by the Governor in Council but only in the circumstances prescribed in those regulations.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is replaced by the schedule set out in Schedule 5 to this Act.

Subsection.Text: Section 168 comes into force 60 days after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Sections 169 and 170 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 127.1(1) of the Customs Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister, or any officer designated by the President for the purposes of this section, may cancel a seizure made under section 110, cancel or reduce a penalty assessed under section 109.3 or an amount demanded under section 124 or refund an amount received under any of sections 117 to 119 within 90 days after the seizure, assessment or demand, if

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 129(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The following persons may, within 90 days after the date of a seizure or the service of a notice, request a decision of the Minister under section 131 by giving notice to the Minister in writing or by any other means that is satisfactory to the Minister:

Section.Text: Subsection 138(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person may apply for a decision by giving notice to the Minister in writing or by any other means that is satisfactory to the Minister.

Section.Text: The definition Board in section 3 of the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency Act is repealed.
The heading before section 18 and sections 18 and 19 of the Act are repealed.
Subsections 21(2) and (2.1) of the Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: The members of the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Board, established by section 18 of the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency Act, cease to hold office on the day on which this Division comes into force.

Despite the provisions of any contract, agreement or order, no person who is appointed to hold office as a member of that Board has any right to claim or receive any compensation, damages, indemnity or other form of relief from Her Majesty in right of Canada or from any employee or agent of Her Majesty for ceasing to hold that office or for the abolition of that office by operation of this Division.

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in sections 180 to 186.

Definition.Text: Agency means the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency established by section 10 of the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency Act.
Corporation means the Enterprise Cape Breton Corporation continued under section 27 of the Enterprise Cape Breton Corporation Act.
Minister means the Minister as defined in section 3 of the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency Act.

Section.Text: The Corporation is dissolved on the day on which this Division comes into force.

Subsection.Text: On the day on which this Division comes into force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Corporation’s assets and those of its subsidiaries, other than the real property described in paragraph (c), are transferred to the Agency;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Corporation’s obligations and those of its subsidiaries, other than those described in paragraph (d), are assumed by the Agency;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the administration of all real property in which the Corporation has any right, title or interest is transferred to the Minister of Public Works and Government Services; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Corporation’s obligations and those of its subsidiaries to former employees of the Cape Breton Development Corporation established by the Cape Breton Development Corporation Act that were acquired by the Corporation or its subsidiaries on December 31, 2009 are assumed by Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the Minister of Public Works and Government Services.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Credits transferred — Department of Public Works and Government Services
Subsection.Text: Any money that is appropriated by an Act of Parliament, for the fiscal year in which this Division comes into force, to defray any of the Corporation’s expenditures related to any real property described in paragraph (1)(c) and any of the Corporation’s expenditures related to any of its obligations described in paragraph (1)(d), and that is unexpended, is deemed to have been appropriated to defray any expenditures of the Department of Public Works and Government Services.

Any money, other than the money referred to in subsection (2), that is appropriated by an Act of Parliament, for the fiscal year in which this Division comes into force, to defray any of the Corporation’s expenditures, and that is unexpended, is deemed to have been appropriated to defray any of the Agency’s expenditures.

Despite subsection 15(1) of the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency Act, every employee of the Corporation or of any of its subsidiaries, other than an employee described in subsection (2), is deemed, on the day on which this Division comes into force, to be a person appointed under the Public Service Employment Act to a position in the Agency and to be an employee as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Public Service Employment Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Appointment to Department of Public Works and Government Services
Subsection.Text: Every employee of the Corporation or of any of its subsidiaries whose functions relate to any real property or obligations described in paragraph 181(1)(c) or (d), as the case may be, is deemed, on the day on which this Division comes into force, to be a person appointed under the Public Service Employment Act to a position in the Department of Public Works and Government Services and to be an employee as defined in subsection 2(1) of that Act.
Every employee referred to in subsection (1) or (2) is entitled

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to receive in the position referred to in subsection (1) or (2) the same rate of pay that they were receiving as an employee of the Corporation or of any of its subsidiaries for as long as that rate of pay is higher than the rate of pay provided for by the terms and conditions of employment that apply or the terms and conditions of employment set out in the collective agreement that applies, as the case may be, to that position;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to retain any vacation and sick leave credits that they had earned during their employment at the Corporation or at any of its subsidiaries; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to be credited with their accumulated years of service at the Corporation, at a subsidiary of the Corporation or at the Cape Breton Development Corporation established by the Cape Breton Development Corporation Act, for the purpose of determining their annual vacation entitlement in the public service.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: In all other respects, the employee is governed by the terms and conditions of employment that apply or the terms and conditions of employment set out in the collective agreement that applies, as the case may be, to that position.

Subsection.Text: The members of the Board of Directors of the Corporation cease to hold office on the day on which this Division comes into force.

Despite the provisions of any contract, agreement or order, no person who is appointed to hold office as a member of the Corporation’s Board of Directors, except the Chief Executive Officer, has any right to claim or receive any compensation, damages, indemnity or other form of relief from Her Majesty in right of Canada or from any employee or agent of Her Majesty for ceasing to hold that office or for the abolition of that office by the operation of this Division.

Section.Text: Subsections 91(1) and (3) of the Financial Administration Act do not apply in respect of the dissolution of, or the sale or other disposal of the assets of, any of the Corporation’s subsidiaries.
After the Corporation is dissolved, the Minister may do any thing and perform any act that is necessary for or incidental to closing out the Corporation’s affairs and those of its subsidiaries.

Any action, suit or other legal proceeding to which the Corporation or any of its subsidiaries is party that is pending in any court on the day on which this Division comes into force may be continued by or against Her Majesty in right of Canada in the same manner and to the same extent as it could have been continued by or against the Corporation or subsidiary, as the case may be.
Paragraph 13(h.1) of the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h.1)
Paragraph.Text: acquire the assets and assume the obligations of the Enterprise Cape Breton Corporation or any of that Corporation’s subsidiaries;

Paragraph.Label: (h.2)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of any security interest that it acquires from the Enterprise Cape Breton Corporation or any of that Corporation’s subsidiaries and that was held by that Corporation or any of those subsidiaries in respect of a loan, investment or agreement that was acquired by that Corporation or any of those subsidiaries,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: hold any security interest,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: surrender any security interest,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: realize any security interest, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: exchange, sell, assign or otherwise dispose of any security interest;

Paragraph.Label: (h.3)
Paragraph.Text: hold or exercise, or surrender, sell, assign or otherwise dispose of, a stock option, share or other similar financial instrument that it acquires from the Enterprise Cape Breton Corporation or any of that Corporation’s subsidiaries;

Paragraph.Label: (h.4)
Paragraph.Text: do all things, other than those mentioned in paragraphs (h.2) and (h.3), that are necessary for or incidental to the administration, management, control or disposal of the assets and obligations that it acquires or assumes from the Enterprise Cape Breton Corporation or any of that Corporation’s subsidiaries; and

Section.Text: Paragraph 89.1(3)(b) of the Financial Administration Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a direction given under subsection 5(2) of the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation Act, subsection 9(2) of the Canadian Commercial Corporation Act or subsection 11(1) of the Canadian Dairy Commission Act; or

Section.Text: Part I of Schedule III to the Act is amended by striking out the following:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Société d’expansion du Cap-Breton

TitleText.XRefExternal: Payments in Lieu of Taxes Act

Section.Text: Schedule III to the Payments in Lieu of Taxes Act is amended by striking out the following:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Société d’expansion du Cap-Breton

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Public Sector Compensation Act is amended by striking out the following:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Société d’expansion du Cap-Breton

Section.Text: The Enterprise Cape Breton Corporation Act, Part II of chapter 41 of the 4th Supplement to the Revised Statues of Canada, 1985, is repealed.
Section 9 of the Museums Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The Canadian Museum of History may support other museums or organizations that have a purpose that is complementary to its own by administering programs that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide online content; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: support the development of online content, including by providing financial assistance.

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in sections 195 to 204.

Definition.Text: Minister means the Minister of Canadian Heritage.
Museum means the Canadian Museum of History.

Section.Text: In sections 196 to 199, program means the program known as the “Online Works of Reference”.
On the day on which this section comes into force, the responsibility for the administration of the program is transferred from the Minister to the Museum.

Any money that is appropriated by an Act of Parliament for the fiscal year in which this section comes into force to defray any expenditures related to the program and that, on the day on which this section comes into force, is unexpended is deemed, on that day, to have been appropriated to defray any of the Museum’s expenditures related to the program.
On the day on which this section comes into force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister’s obligations that relate to the program are assumed by the Museum;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contracts entered into by the Minister that relate to the program and are still in force are deemed to have been entered into by the Museum;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: permits, licences and other authorizations issued to the Minister that relate exclusively to the program and are still valid are transferred to the Museum; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: permits, licences and other authorizations issued by the Minister that relate to the program and are still valid are deemed to have been issued by the Museum.

Section.Text: On the day on which this section comes into force, the assets of the Department of Canadian Heritage that relate exclusively to the program are transferred to the Museum.
In sections 201 to 204, program means the program known as the “Virtual Museum of Canada”.
On the day on which this section comes into force, the responsibility for the administration of the program is transferred from the Minister to the Museum.

Any money that is appropriated by an Act of Parliament for the fiscal year in which this section comes into force to defray any expenditures related to the program and that, on the day on which this section comes into force, is unexpended is deemed, on that day, to have been appropriated to defray any of the Museum’s expenditures related to the program.
On the day on which this section comes into force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister’s obligations that relate to the program are assumed by the Museum;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contracts entered into by the Minister that relate to the program and are still in force are deemed to have been entered into by the Museum;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: permits, licences and other authorizations issued to the Minister that relate exclusively to the program and are still valid are transferred to the Museum; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: permits, licences and other authorizations issued by the Minister that relate to the program and are still valid are deemed to have been issued by the Museum.

Section.Text: On the day on which this section comes into force, the assets of the Department of Canadian Heritage that relate exclusively to the program are transferred to the Museum.
The provisions of this Division come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Nordion and Theratronics Divestiture Authorization Act

Section.Text: The definitions Nordion and Theratronics in subsection 2(1) of the Nordion and Theratronics Divestiture Authorization Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Nordion means Nordion International Inc., a corporation incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act or any successor to that corporation;
Theratronics means Theratronics International Limited, a corporation incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act or any successor to that corporation.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(3) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: by any person if the acquisition of those shares by that person

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is an investment that, under sections 21 to 23 of the Investment Canada Act, is, or is deemed, likely to be of net benefit to Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is not prohibited under Part IV.1 of that Act.

Subsection.Text: Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

No provision imposing constraints under subsection (1) and no regulation referred to in subsection (2) apply in respect of any voting shares of Nordion referred to in paragraph (3)(c) that are subsequently held by any other person.

Section.Text: Section 7 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 7(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply if a person holds voting shares referred to in paragraph 6(3)(c) or subsection 6(3.1).

Section.Text: The portion of section 8 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Sections 5 to 7, with the exception of paragraph 6(3)(c) and subsections 6(3.1) and 7(2), apply to Theratronics as if
The Bank Act is amended by adding the following after section 415.1:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting a bank’s activities in relation to derivatives.

In this section, derivative means an option, swap, futures contract, forward contract or other financial or commodity contract or instrument whose market price, value, delivery obligations, payment obligations or settlement obligations are derived from, referenced to or based on an underlying interest, including a price, rate, index, value, variable, event, probability or thing.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting a bank’s activities in relation to benchmarks.
In this section, benchmark means a price, estimate, rate, index or value that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: determined from time to time by reference to an assessment of one or more underlying interests;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: made available to the public, either free of charge or on payment; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: used for reference for any purpose, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: determining the interest payable, or other sums that are due, under loan agreements or other financial contracts or instruments,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: determining the value of financial instruments or the price at which they may be bought or sold, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: measuring the performance of financial instruments.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 237(2) of the Insurance Companies Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.01)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the process that precedes the calling of a special meeting under subsection (1.1), including the development of a conversion proposal, and respecting the calling of that meeting;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 237(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: governing the ownership of shares issued by a mutual company that has been converted into a company with common shares, including limiting the circumstances in which the Minister may give approval under subsection 407(1);

Subsection.Text: Section 237 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Regulations made under paragraph (2)(a.01) may provide for court intervention in the process referred to in that paragraph, including the circumstances in which the court is to be seized of any matter in relation to that process, and may govern the court’s powers and procedures in that regard. They may also govern authorizations by the Superintendent in respect of notices to be sent in the context of that process.

Section.Text: The long title of the French version of the Motor Vehicle Safety Act is replaced by the following:

AmendedText.LongTitle: Loi régissant la fabrication et l’importation des véhicules et équipements automobiles en vue de limiter les risques de mort, de blessures et de dommages matériels et environnementaux

Subsection.Text: The definitions national safety mark, standard and vehicle in section 2 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: national safety mark means a prescribed expression, symbol or abbreviation or any combination of them;

standard means a standard that governs the design, manufacture, functioning or marking of vehicles or equipment for the purpose of reducing, directly or indirectly, the risk of death, injury or property damage from vehicle use, including a standard aimed at increasing the use of safety features by the public or facilitating the creation, recording or retrieval of information;

vehicle means any vehicle that is designed to be, or is capable of being, driven or drawn on roads by any means other than muscular power exclusively, but does not include any vehicle that is designed to run exclusively on rails.

Subsection.Text: The definition fabrication ou construction in section 2 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: fabrication ou construction S’agissant d’un véhicule, ensemble des opérations de réalisation de celui-ci y compris les modifications qui y sont apportées, jusqu’à sa vente au premier usager.

Section.Text: Subsection 3(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company authorized by the Minister, as provided for in the regulations, may apply a national safety mark to a vehicle or equipment, as provided for in the regulations.
The company shall provide the Minister with the address of the premises at which the national safety mark is to be applied.

Section.Text: The heading before section 5 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 5(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: evidence of that conformity has been obtained and produced as provided for in the regulations or, if the regulations so provide, to the Minister’s satisfaction;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 5(1)(d) to (h) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: prescribed information is marked on the vehicle or equipment as provided for in the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: prescribed documentation or prescribed accessories accompany the vehicle or equipment as provided for in the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: prescribed information relating to the operation of the vehicle or equipment is disseminated as provided for in the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: records are maintained and furnished, as provided for in the regulations, in relation to the design, manufacture, testing and field performance of the vehicle or equipment, for the purpose of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: enabling an inspector to determine whether the vehicle or equipment conforms to all requirements applicable to it, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: facilitating the identification and analysis of defects referred to in subsection 10(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of equipment, the company maintains, as provided for in the regulations, a registration system by which any person who has purchased equipment manufactured, imported or sold by the company and who wishes to be identified may be so identified.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5(2) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 7(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before the importation, the person importing the vehicle or equipment makes a declaration, as provided for in the regulations, that the vehicle or equipment

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: will be used in Canada solely for a prescribed purpose,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: will remain in Canada for a period of not more than one year or any other period that is specified by the Minister, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: meets or will meet any other prescribed requirement; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 7(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the vehicle or equipment is passing through Canada to another country or is exclusively for use by a visitor to Canada.

Subsection.Text: Section 7 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

A person who imports a vehicle or equipment under paragraph (1)(a) shall, as provided for in the regulations, export or destroy the vehicle or equipment before the end of the period referred to in subparagraph (1)(a)(ii).
Despite subsection (1.01), a person who imports a vehicle under paragraph (1)(a) may, with the Minister’s approval, donate it as provided for in the regulations.
Subsection 7(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vehicles from the United States or Mexico
Subsection.Text: Sections 5 and 6 do not apply to the importation of a vehicle that has been sold at the retail level in the United States or that is a prescribed vehicle from Mexico if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the vehicle meets any prescribed requirements; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person importing the vehicle makes a declaration, as provided for in the regulations, that the vehicle will be, before being presented for registration by a province and within the prescribed period,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: brought into compliance with any prescribed requirements, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: certified, as provided for in the regulations, as compliant with the requirements referred to in subparagraph (i) by the person designated in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Sections 5 and 6 do not apply to the importation of a vehicle that has been sold at the retail level in the United States or that is a prescribed vehicle from Mexico if the person importing the vehicle makes a declaration, as provided for in the regulations, that the vehicle

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: will not be presented for registration by a province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: will be registered, as provided for in the regulations, by the person designated in the regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: will be dismantled for its components.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7(4) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 7(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
A person who makes a declaration under this section shall comply with the declaration.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

A company that applies a national safety mark to any vehicle or equipment or that imports any vehicle or equipment of a class for which standards are prescribed shall, on the Minister’ request, provide the Minister with the means to retrieve and analyse information created or recorded by the vehicle or equipment.
The portion of subsection 9(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On application by a company as provided for in the regulations, the Governor in Council may, by order, grant an exemption for a specified period, in accordance with any conditions specified in the order, for any model of vehicle manufactured or imported by the company from conformity with any prescribed standard if conformity with that standard would, in the opinion of the Governor in Council,

Section.Text: The heading before section 10 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: NOTICE OF DEFECT AND OF NON-COMPLIANCE

Subsection.Text: Subsections 10(1) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

A company that applies a national safety mark to any vehicle or equipment, sells any vehicle or equipment to which a national safety mark has been applied or imports any vehicle or equipment of a class for which standards are prescribed shall, as provided for in the regulations, give notice of any defect in the design, manufacture or functioning of the vehicle or equipment that affects or is likely to affect the safety of any person to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister, on becoming aware of the defect; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the current owner and any other prescribed person, within the period provided for in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: A company is not required to give notice of a defect

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for which notice has already been given by another company that manufactured, sold or imported the vehicle or equipment; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for which a notice of non-compliance has been given under section 10.1.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister determines that a defect has not been corrected in an adequate number of vehicles or equipment for which notice was given under subsection (1), the Minister may, by order, require the company to provide, in accordance with any conditions specified in the order, a subsequent notice to those persons who have not had the defect corrected. In making this determination, the Minister shall take into consideration, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature of the defect;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the safety risk arising from it; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total number of vehicles or equipment affected.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is satisfied that the current owner of a vehicle or equipment cannot reasonably be determined by a company,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the company shall give notice in any other manner that is acceptable to the Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister may exempt the company from the requirement to give notice to the current owner.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, require any company that applies a national safety mark to any vehicle or equipment, sells any vehicle or equipment to which a national safety mark has been applied or imports any vehicle or equipment of a class for which standards are prescribed to give notice of a defect in the manner specified in the order, if the Minister considers that it is in the interest of safety.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection (2.1) or (4).
Subsections 10(6) and (7) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10:

Subsection.Text: A company that applies a national safety mark to any vehicle or equipment, sells any vehicle or equipment to which a national safety mark has been applied or imports any vehicle or equipment of a class for which standards are prescribed shall, as provided for in the regulations, give notice of any non-compliance of the vehicle or equipment with the regulations to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister, on becoming aware of the non-compliance; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the current owner and any other prescribed person, within the period provided for in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: A company is not required to give notice of any non-compliance for which notice has already been given by another company that manufactured, sold or imported the vehicle or equipment.
If the Minister determines that the non-compliance is inconsequential to safety, the company is not required to give notice under paragraph (1)(b).

If the Minister determines that a non-compliance has not been corrected in an adequate number of vehicles or equipment, the Minister may, by order, require a company to provide, in accordance with any conditions specified in the order, a subsequent notice to those persons who have not had the non-compliance corrected. In making this determination, the Minister shall take into consideration, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature of the non-compliance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the safety risk arising from it; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total number of vehicles or equipment affected.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is satisfied that the current owner of a vehicle or equipment cannot reasonably be determined by a company

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the company shall give notice in any other manner that is acceptable to the Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister may exempt the company from the requirement to give notice to the current owner.

Subsection.Text: On receiving a notice under subsection (1), the Minister shall forward full particulars of it to the minister or other officer who is responsible for motor vehicle administration in each province.

The Minister may, by order, require any company that applies a national safety mark to any vehicle or equipment, sells any vehicle or equipment to which a national safety mark has been applied or imports any vehicle or equipment of a class for which standards are prescribed to give a notice of non-compliance in the manner specified by the Minister, if the Minister considers that it is in the interest of safety.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection (4) or (7).

Section.Text: A company that gives notice to the Minister shall submit reports to the Minister as provided for in the regulations.
A company that gives notice to the Minister in respect of a vehicle shall, as provided for in regulations, make information available for the vehicle in respect of which the notice was given.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 11(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the keeping of records and the provision of information to the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing or providing for anything that by this Act is to be prescribed or provided for by the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 11(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:
Regulations made under this section may, in whole or in part, incorporate by reference, as it is amended from time to time or as it exists on a particular date,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a document produced by a person or body other than the Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a technical or explanatory document produced by the Minister including specifications, classifications, illustrations, graphs, test methods, procedures, operational standards and performance standards.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, a document that is incorporated by reference in a regulation is not required to be transmitted for registration or published in the Canada Gazette under the Statutory Instruments Act by reason only that it is incorporated by reference.

Section.Text: Section 12 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In this section, technical standards document means a document that is published by the Minister, as provided for in the regulations, that adapts, or that reproduces in whole or in part in the official languages of Canada, an enactment of a foreign government or material produced by an international organization. The adaptations may include amendments to the content of the originating enactment or material.
Regulations made under this Act may, in whole or in part, incorporate by reference a technical standards document as it is amended from time to time or as it exists on a particular date.

For greater certainty, a technical standards document that is incorporated by reference in a regulation is not required to be transmitted for registration or published in the Canada Gazette under the Statutory Instruments Act by reason only that it is incorporated by reference.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, subsections 11(3) and 12(2) do not limit any authority to make regulations incorporating material by reference that exists apart from those subsections.
Section 14 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: An inspector may not be compelled to give testimony in any civil suit, with regard to information obtained by them in the discharge of their duties, without the Minister’s written permission.

Section.Text: Paragraph 15(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any vehicle or equipment of a class for which standards have been prescribed that is owned by, or is on the premises of, a company or a consignee of imported vehicles or imported equipment;

Section.Text: Subsections 17(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every corporation or company that contravenes this Act, the regulations or an order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to a fine of not more than $200,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to a fine of not more than $2 million.

Subsection.Text: Every individual who contravenes this Act, the regulations or an order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to a fine of not more than $4,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to a fine of not more than $20,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years, or to both.

Section.Text: The heading before section 20 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 20(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: conduct any research, studies, evaluations and analyses that the Minister considers necessary for the administration and enforcement of this Act;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 20(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by replacing paragraph (e) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: collect any information related to vehicles or equipment that the Minister considers to be in the public interest; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: publish or otherwise disseminate any information, other than personal information, relating to the activities of the Minister under this section.

Subsection.Text: Section 20 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
For the purposes of paragraphs (1)(a), (b) and (e), the Minister may collect personal information, as defined in section 3 of the Privacy Act, including personal information from third parties.

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Act is repealed.
Section 50 of the Railway Safety Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Subsections 30(1) and (2) of the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992 are repealed.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Ensuring Safe Vehicles Imported From Mexico For Canadians Act

Section.Text: Sections 2 and 3 of the Ensuring Safe Vehicles Imported from Mexico for Canadians Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 51(1) of the Safe Food for Canadians Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting or prohibiting the purchasing or receiving of any fresh fruit or vegetable that is imported or that is sent or conveyed from one province to another;

Subsection.Text: Section 51 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Regulations made under paragraph (1)(d), (e) or (e.1) in respect of any fresh fruit or vegetable may, among other things, require a person to be a member of an entity or organization specified in the regulations.

Section.Text: The headings before section 60 and sections 60 to 67 of the Act are repealed.
Section 86 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 28(1)(a) of the Federal Courts Act is repealed.
Paragraph 28(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Review Tribunal continued by subsection 27(1) of the Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Act;

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Safe Food for Canadians Act.
If section 86 of the other Act comes into force before section 236 of this Act, then on the day on which that section 236 comes into force, paragraph 28(1)(a) of the Federal Courts Act is repealed.
If section 236 of this Act comes into force on the same day as section 86 of the other Act, then that section 236 is deemed to have come into force before that section 86.

Section.Text: Section 213, subsections 217(1), (3), (4) and (6) and sections 220 to 222 and 230 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 27(3) of the Telecommunications Act is replaced by the following:

The Commission may determine in any case, as a question of fact, whether a Canadian carrier has complied with this section or section 25, 27.1 or 29, or with any decision made under section 24, 25, 29, 34 or 40.
Subsection 27(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commission may determine in any case, as a question of fact, whether a Canadian carrier has complied with this section or section 25 or 29, or with any decision made under section 24, 25, 29, 34 or 40.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 27:

The amount charged during a year by a Canadian carrier to a second Canadian carrier for roaming services with respect to the transmission of all domestic wireless voice calls and the domestic portion of all international wireless voice calls shall not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the first Canadian carrier’s total retail revenues from the provision of wireless voice call services to its Canadian subscribers, for calls both originating and terminating in Canada, for the preceding year; and
is the number of minutes provided for those calls for the preceding year.

Subsection.Text: The amount charged during a year by a Canadian carrier to a second Canadian carrier for roaming services with respect to the transmission of wireless data in Canada shall not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the first Canadian carrier’s total retail revenues from the provision of wireless data services in Canada to its Canadian subscribers for the preceding year; and
is the number of megabytes provided for those data services for the preceding year.

Subsection.Text: The amount charged during a year by a Canadian carrier to a second Canadian carrier for roaming services with respect to the transmission of all domestic wireless text messages and the domestic portion of all international wireless text messages shall not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the first Canadian carrier’s total retail revenues from the provision of wireless text message services to its Canadian subscribers, for text messages both originating and terminating in Canada, for the preceding year; and
is the number of those text messages for the preceding year.

Subsection.Text: The Canadian carrier shall not charge the second Canadian carrier any other amount in relation to the provision of the roaming services referred to in subsections (1) to (3).

The amount established by the Commission that a Canadian carrier can charge to a second Canadian carrier for roaming services prevails over an amount determined under any of subsections (1) to (3) to the extent of any inconsistency.
Section 27.1 of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Subsections 239(2) and 240(2) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsection 206.3(6) of the Canada Labour Code is repealed.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 207:
Subject to the regulations, a leave of absence under any of sections 206.3 to 206.5 may only be taken in one or more periods of not less than one week’s duration.

Subsection.Text: An employee may interrupt a leave of absence referred to in any of sections 206.3 to 206.5 in order to be absent due to a reason referred to in subsection 239(1) or 239.1(1).
The interrupted leave resumes immediately after the interruption ends.

Except to the extent that it is inconsistent with subsection 239(1.1), section 209.1 applies to an employee who interrupted the leave in order to be absent due to a reason referred to in subsection 239(1).

Except to the extent that it is inconsistent with subsections 239.1(3) and (4), section 209.1 applies to an employee who interrupted the leave in order to be absent due to a reason referred to in subsection 239.1(1).

Section.Text: Section 207.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Notice to employer — interruption of leave

Subsection.Text: An employee who intends to interrupt their leave under subsection 206.1(2.4) or 207.02(1) shall provide the employer with a notice in writing of the interruption before or as soon as possible after it begins.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice to employer — resumption of leave
Subsection.Text: The employee shall provide the employer with a notice in writing of the day on which they resume their leave before or as soon as possible after that day.
Subsection 207.3(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every employee who takes a leave of absence from employment under any of sections 206.3 to 206.5 shall, as soon as possible, provide the employer with a notice in writing of the reasons for the leave and the length of the leave that they intend to take.
Subsection 207.3(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of change in length of leave
Subsection.Text: Every employee who is on a leave of absence from employment under any of sections 206.3 to 206.5 shall, as soon as possible, provide the employer with a notice in writing of any change in the length of the leave that they intend to take.
Subsections 207.3(3) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Leave of more than four weeks
Subsection.Text: If the length of the leave taken under section 206.4 or 206.5 is more than four weeks, the notice in writing of any change in the length of the leave shall be provided on at least four weeks’ notice, unless there is a valid reason why that cannot be done.

The employer may require the employee to provide documentation in support of the reasons for the leave taken under section 206.4 or 206.5 and of any change in the length of leave that the employee intends to take.

If an employee who takes a leave of more than four weeks under section 206.4 or 206.5 wishes to shorten the length of the leave but does not provide the employer with four weeks’ notice, then the employer may postpone the employee’s return to work for a period of up to four weeks after the day on which the employee informs the employer of the new end date of the leave. If the employer informs the employee that their return to work is postponed, the employee is not entitled to return to work until the day that is indicated by the employer.

Section.Text: Section 209.4 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.1):

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the maximum number of periods of leave of absence that an employee may take under any of sections 206.3 to 206.5;

Section.Text: Subsection 18(2) of the Employment Insurance Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A claimant to whom benefits are payable under any of sections 23 to 23.2 is not disentitled under paragraph (1)(b) for failing to prove that he or she would have been available for work were it not for the illness, injury or quarantine.

Section.Text: Subsection 152.03(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A self-employed person to whom benefits are payable under any of sections 152.05 to 152.061 is entitled to benefits under subsection (1) even though the person did not cease to work as a self-employed person because of a prescribed illness, injury or quarantine and would not be working even without the illness, injury or quarantine.

Section.Text: Subsection 152.09(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: providing care or support to one or more critically ill children of the self-employed person.

Section.Text: Subsections 18(2) and 152.03(1.1) of the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by sections 247 and 248, apply only to claims for benefits because of illness, injury or quarantine that are made for weeks that begin on or after the day on which sections 247 and 248 come into force.
This Division, other than section 249, comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act is amended by adding the following after section 25:

The User Fees Act does not apply to a fee fixed under section 24 for a service or the use of a facility provided by the Agency under the Safe Food for Canadians Act or to a fee fixed under section 25 in respect of products, rights and privileges provided by the Agency under that Act.
This Division comes into force on the day on which section 103 of the Safe Food for Canadians Act comes into force or, if it is later, on the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act

Section.Text: The definition officer in section 2 of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act is repealed.
Subparagraph 3(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: establishing an agency that is responsible for ensuring compliance with Parts 1 and 1.1 and for dealing with reported and other information;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 5(g) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: persons and entities authorized under provincial legislation to engage in the business of dealing in securities or any other financial instruments or to provide portfolio management or investment advising services, other than persons who act exclusively on behalf of such an authorized person or entity;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 5(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: persons and entities that have a place of business in Canada and that are engaged in the business of providing at least one of the following services:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: foreign exchange dealing,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: remitting funds or transmitting funds by any means or through any person, entity or electronic funds transfer network,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: issuing or redeeming money orders, traveller’s cheques or other similar negotiable instruments except for cheques payable to a named person or entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: dealing in virtual currencies, as defined in the regulations, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: any service described in regulations made under paragraph 73(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (h.1)
Paragraph.Text: persons and entities that do not have a place of business in Canada, that are engaged in the business of providing at least one of the following services that is directed at persons or entities in Canada, and that provide those services to their customers in Canada:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: foreign exchange dealing,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: remitting funds or transmitting funds by any means or through any person, entity or electronic funds transfer network,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: issuing or redeeming money orders, traveller’s cheques or other similar negotiable instruments except for cheques payable to a named person or entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: dealing in virtual currencies, as defined in the regulations, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: any service described in regulations made under paragraph 73(1)(a);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 5(k) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the government of a province that, in accordance with paragraph 207(1)(a) of the Criminal Code,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in a permanent establishment that is held out to be a casino, conducts and manages a lottery scheme that includes games of roulette or card games, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other permanent establishment, conducts and manages games that are operated on or through a slot machine, as defined in subsection 198(3) of the Criminal Code, or any other similar electronic gaming device, if there are more than 50 of those machines or other devices in the establishment;

Paragraph.Label: (k.1)
Paragraph.Text: the government of a province that, in accordance with paragraph 207(1)(a) of the Criminal Code, conducts and manages a lottery scheme, other than bingo or the sale of lottery tickets, that is accessible to the public through the Internet or other digital network, except if the network is an internal network within an establishment described in subparagraph (k)(ii);

Paragraph.Label: (k.2)
Paragraph.Text: an organization that, in accordance with paragraph 207(1)(b) of the Criminal Code, in a permanent establishment that is held out to be a casino, conducts and manages a lottery scheme that includes games of roulette or card games, unless the organization is a registered charity, as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act, and the lottery scheme is conducted or managed for a period of not more than two consecutive days at a time;

Paragraph.Label: (k.3)
Paragraph.Text: the board of a fair or of an exhibition, or the operator of a concession leased by such a board, that, in accordance with paragraph 207(1)(c) of the Criminal Code, in a permanent establishment that is held out to be a casino, conducts and manages a lottery scheme that includes games of roulette or card games;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:
For greater certainty, this Part does not apply to persons or entities referred to in paragraph 5(h.1) in respect of the services they provide to persons or entities outside Canada.
Section 9.3 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person or entity that is referred to in section 5 and that is prescribed shall determine, in the prescribed circumstances and in accordance with the regulations, whether it is dealing with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a politically exposed foreign person, a prescribed family member of a politically exposed foreign person, or a person who the person or entity knows or should reasonably know is closely associated, for personal or business reasons, with a politically exposed foreign person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a politically exposed domestic person, a prescribed family member of a politically exposed domestic person, or a person who the person or entity knows or should reasonably know is closely associated, for personal or business reasons, with a politically exposed domestic person; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the head of an international organization, a prescribed family member of the head of an international organization, or a person who the person or entity knows or should reasonably know is closely associated, for personal or business reasons, with the head of an international organization.

Subsection.Text: If the person or entity determines that it is dealing with a person described in paragraph (1)(a), the person or entity shall obtain the approval of senior management in the prescribed circumstances and take the prescribed measures.

If the person or entity determines that it is dealing with a person described in paragraph (1)(b) or (c) and considers, based on an assessment referred to in subsection 9.6(2), that the person poses a high risk of committing a money laundering offence or a terrorist activity financing offence, the person or entity shall obtain the approval of senior management in the prescribed circumstances and take the prescribed measures.
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: head of an international organization means the head of an international organization that is established by the governments of states or the head of an institution of any such organization.

politically exposed domestic person means a person who, at a given time, holds — or has held within a prescribed period before that time — one of the offices or positions referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (j) in or on behalf of the federal government or a provincial government or the office or position referred to in paragraph (k) in a municipal government:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Governor General, lieutenant governor or head of government;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: member of the Senate or House of Commons or member of a legislature;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: deputy minister or equivalent rank;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: ambassador, or attaché or counsellor of an ambassador;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: military officer with a rank of general or above;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: president of a corporation that is wholly owned directly by Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: head of a government agency;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: judge of an appellate court in a province, the Federal Court of Appeal or the Supreme Court of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: leader or president of a political party represented in a legislature;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: holder of any prescribed office or position; or

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: mayor.

Definition.Text: politically exposed foreign person means a person who holds or has held one of the following offices or positions in or on behalf of a foreign state:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: head of state or head of government;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: member of the executive council of government or member of a legislature;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: deputy minister or equivalent rank;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: ambassador, or attaché or counsellor of an ambassador;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: military officer with a rank of general or above;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: president of a state-owned company or a state-owned bank;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: head of a government agency;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: judge of a supreme court, constitutional court or other court of last resort;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: leader or president of a political party represented in a legislature; or

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: holder of any prescribed office or position.

Subsection.Text: No entity referred to in paragraph 5(a), (b), (d) or (e) and no other entity that is referred to in section 5 and that is prescribed shall open or maintain an account for, or have a correspondent banking relationship with, a person or entity referred to in paragraph 5(h.1) unless that person or entity is registered with the Centre under section 11.1.

For the purposes of this section, correspondent banking relationship means a relationship created by an agreement or arrangement under which an entity referred to in paragraph 5(a), (b), (d) or (e) or an entity that is referred to in section 5 and that is prescribed undertakes to provide to a person or entity referred to in paragraph 5(h.1) services such as international electronic funds transfers, cash management, cheque clearing and any prescribed services.

Section.Text: Subsection 9.4(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No person or entity shall have a correspondent banking relationship with a shell bank as defined in the regulations.

Section.Text: Sections 9.7 and 9.8 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every entity referred to in any of paragraphs 5(a) to (g), except for authorized foreign banks within the meaning of section 2 of the Bank Act and for foreign companies within the meaning of section 2 of the Insurance Companies Act, shall, in respect of its foreign branches, and in respect of its foreign subsidiaries that carry out activities similar to those of entities referred to in those paragraphs and that are either wholly-owned by the entity or have financial statements that are consolidated with those of the entity, develop policies that establish requirements similar to the requirements of sections 6, 6.1 and 9.6 and ensure that those branches and subsidiaries apply those policies to the extent it is permitted by, and does not conflict with, the laws of the country in which the branch or subsidiary is located.
Before the policies referred to in subsection (1) are applied, the entity’s board of directors, if there is one, shall approve them.
Subsection (1) does not apply to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an entity that is a subsidiary of an entity to which that subsection applies; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an entity that is a subsidiary of a foreign entity that has developed policies that establish requirements for its subsidiaries that are similar to the requirements of sections 6, 6.1 and 9.6, if that subsidiary is applying those policies to the extent it is permitted by, and do not conflict with, the laws of Canada or a province.

Subsection.Text: If the application by a foreign branch or a foreign subsidiary of a policy referred to in subsection (1) is not permitted by or would conflict with the laws of the country in which the branch or subsidiary is located, the entity shall keep and retain, in accordance with section 6, a record of that fact and of the reasons why it is not permitted or it would conflict, and shall, within a reasonable time, notify the Centre, and the principal agency or body that supervises or regulates it under federal or provincial law, of that fact and those reasons.

Every entity referred to in any of paragraphs 5(a) to (g) that is affiliated with an entity referred to in those paragraphs or with a foreign entity that carries out activities similar to those of entities referred to in those paragraphs shall develop and apply policies and procedures related to the exchange of information between the entity and those affiliated entities for the purpose of detecting or deterring a money laundering offence or a terrorist activity financing offence or of assessing the risk of such an offence.

For the purposes of subsection (1), an entity is affiliated with another entity if one of them is wholly owned by the other, if both are wholly owned by the same entity or if their financial statements are consolidated.

Section.Text: Section 11.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Except as otherwise provided in the regulations, every person or entity referred to in paragraph 5(h) or (h.1), those referred to in paragraph 5(l) that sell money orders to the public, and every other person or entity that is referred to in section 5 and that is prescribed must be registered with the Centre in accordance with this section and sections 11.11 to 11.2.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 11.11(1)(c)(i) to (iv) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a money laundering offence, or an offence under the laws of a foreign country that is substantially similar to a money laundering offence,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a terrorist activity financing offence, or an offence under the laws of a foreign country that is substantially similar to a terrorist activity financing offence,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an offence under this Act or the Proceeds of Crime (money laundering) Act, chapter 26 of the Statutes of Canada, 1991 when convicted on indictment, or an offence under the laws of a foreign country that is substantially similar to an offence under either Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: an offence under any of sections 83.18 to 83.231, 354 or 467.11 to 467.13 of the Criminal Code, or an offence under the laws of a foreign country that is substantially similar to such an offence, or

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 11.11(1)(d) and (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person or entity that has been convicted on indictment or convicted more than once for an offence under any of the following, or that has been convicted of an offence under the laws of a foreign country that is substantially similar to an offence under any of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: Part X of the Criminal Code,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act, except for the offence under subsection 4(1) of that Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 39, 44.2, 44.3, 48, 50.2 or 50.3 of the Food and Drugs Act, as that section read immediately before May 14, 1997, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 4, 5, 6, 19.1 or 19.2 of the Narcotic Control Act, chapter N-1 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1985, as that section read immediately before May 14, 1997;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an entity that is a corporation in respect of which a director, the chief executive officer, the president or a person who owns or controls, directly or indirectly, 20% or more of the shares has been convicted on indictment of an offence under this Act or the Proceeds of Crime (money laundering) Act, chapter 26 of the Statutes of Canada, 1991 or has been convicted of an offence under the laws of a foreign country that is substantially similar to an offence under either Act;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 11.11(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: a person or entity referred to in paragraph 5(h.1) that, having committed a violation, or a series of minor violations, under this Act that is classified as a serious violation or a very serious violation and being liable to a penalty for it, and 30 days having elapsed since the day on which all proceedings in respect of the violation are ended, has still not paid the penalty together with any interest imposed under section 73.28; or

Section.Text: Subsection 11.12(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An application for registration shall be submitted to the Centre in the prescribed form and manner and shall include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a list of the applicant’s agents, mandataries or branches that are engaged, on behalf of the applicant, in the activities referred to in paragraph 5(h) or (h.1), in selling money orders to the public if the applicant is a person or entity referred to in paragraph 5(l), or in any prescribed activities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the applicant is a person referred to in paragraph 5(h.1)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the name and address for service of an individual who resides in Canada and who is authorized to accept, on behalf of the person, notices that are served or caused to be served by the Centre under this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a document that sets out the person’s record of criminal convictions, or states that the person does not have one, that is issued by a competent authority of the foreign state in which the person resides, and, if the document is made in a language other than English or French, a translation of it into one of those languages attested to by a person who is recognized as a certified translator by a provincial organization or body that is competent under provincial law to issue such certifications;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the applicant is an entity referred to in paragraph 5(h.1)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the name and address for service of an individual who resides in Canada and who is authorized to accept, on behalf of the entity, notices that are served or caused to be served by the Centre under this Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for each of the chief executive officer, the president and the directors of the entity and for each person who owns or controls, directly or indirectly, 20% or more of the entity or the shares of the entity, a document that sets out the person’s record of criminal convictions, or states that the person does not have one, and that is issued by a competent authority of the foreign state in which the person resides, and, if the document is made in a language other than English or French, a translation of it into one of those languages attested to by a person who is recognized as a certified translator by a provincial organization or body that is competent under provincial law to issue such certifications; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed information.

Section.Text: Section 11.13 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 11.13(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: If the name or address for service of a person referred to in subparagraph 11.12(1)(b)(i) or (c)(i) changes, and an applicant or registered person or entity who is or was required to provide the information described in paragraph 11.12(1)(b) or (c) does not, within the period provided in subsection (1), provide the Centre with the new name or address for service, the Centre shall without delay after becoming aware of that fact deny the application, or revoke the registration, as the case may be, and shall, without delay, inform the applicant or registered person or entity of the denial or revocation.

Section.Text: Subsection 11.14(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An applicant shall provide the Centre, in the prescribed form and manner, with any clarifications that the Centre may request in respect of the information described in subsection 11.12(1) within 30 days after the day on which the request is made.

Section.Text: Subsection 11.17(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A registered person or entity shall provide the Centre, in the prescribed form and manner, with any clarifications that the Centre may request in respect of the information described in subsection 11.12(1) within 30 days after the day on which the request is made.

Section.Text: Section 11.41 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: foreign entity means an entity, other than an entity referred to in section 5, that is incorporated or formed by or under the laws of a country other than Canada, including its subsidiaries, if any, and that does not carry on business in Canada, if it carries out activities similar to those of entities referred to in any of paragraphs 5(a) to (g) or activities referred to in paragraph 5(h) or (h.1).
foreign state means a country other than Canada and includes any political subdivision or territory of a foreign state.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 11.42(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In addition to any other measure required by this Act, the Minister may, by written directive, require any person or entity referred to in section 5 to take, in order to safeguard the integrity of Canada’s financial system, any measure specified in the directive with respect to any financial transaction, or any financial transaction within a class of financial transactions, originating from or bound for any foreign state or foreign entity, that occurs or is attempted in the course of their activities, or with respect to any activity that is related to any such financial transaction or class of financial transactions.
Section 11.42 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

A requirement in a directive to take a reporting measure as contemplated by paragraph (2)(e) does not apply to persons or entities referred to in paragraph 5(i) or (j) who are, as the case may be, legal counsel or legal firms, when they are providing legal services.

Section.Text: Sections 11.44 and 11.45 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every entity referred to in any of paragraphs 5(a) to (g), except for authorized foreign banks within the meaning of section 2 of the Bank Act and for foreign companies within the meaning of section 2 of the Insurance Companies Act, shall ensure that its foreign branches, and that its foreign subsidiaries that carry out activities similar to those of entities referred to in those paragraphs and that are either wholly-owned by the entity or have financial statements that are consolidated with those of the entity, comply with any directive issued under this Part, except with respect to any reporting measure as contemplated by paragraph 11.42(2)(e), to the extent it is permitted by, and does not conflict with, the laws of the country in which the branch or subsidiary is located.

If compliance with a directive by a branch or a subsidiary is not permitted by or would conflict with the laws of the country in which the branch or subsidiary is located, the entity shall keep and retain, in accordance with section 6, a record of that fact and of the reasons why it is not permitted or it would conflict, and shall, within a reasonable time, notify the Centre, and the principal agency or body that supervises or regulates it under federal or provincial law, of that fact and those reasons.

Section.Text: Section 11.6 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every entity referred to in any of paragraphs 5(a) to (g), except for authorized foreign banks within the meaning of section 2 of the Bank Act and for foreign companies within the meaning of section 2 of the Insurance Companies Act, shall ensure that its foreign branches comply with any regulation made under subsection 11.49(1) to the extent it is permitted by, and does not conflict with, the laws of the country in which the branch is located.
The Act is amended by adding the following before the heading “Reporting” before section 12:
In this Part, officer has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act.
Subsections 12(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person arriving in or departing from Canada shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: answer truthfully any questions asked by the officer in the performance of the officer’s duties and functions under this Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
